tavily.answer: Somalia is experiencing severe drought conditions, with the UN warning that acute hunger is set to worsen across 13 global hotspots, including Somalia, due to conflict, funding shortages, and climate shocks. The French Navy's Charles de Gaulle Carrier Strike Group safely transited the Strait of Bab el Mandeb, a critical maritime route, without incident. Somalia is also facing a famine risk in parts of the country for the first time in four years, with over 37% of young children in the Burhakaba District suffering from acute malnutrition. Additionally, piracy continues to threaten seafarers, with crews of hijacked vessels reporting deteriorating conditions.
